change_history.change: Horologion v1.8 — Sunday Small Vespers implemented as a real baseline path
change_history.detail: js/horologion-engine.js: version bumped v1.7→v1.8. Five Sunday-case variable slots resolved. (1) stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried: _resolveVespersSlots() now routes dayOfWeek===0 to the 'saturday' Octoechos corpus; resolvedAs, weekday, and label fields patched post-resolution to 'sunday-small-vespers-resurrectional-stichera'; rubric note appended stating 3 stichera typically used at Sunday Small Vespers. (2) aposticha: same routing and label-patch logic; resolvedAs:'sunday-small-vespers-resurrectional-aposticha'. Liturgical rationale: the resurrectional stichera are the tone-week corpus, not Saturday-specific — they are anticipated at Saturday Great Vespers and repeated at Sunday Small Vespers; no new data fabricated. (3) kathisma-reading: _resolveKathismaSlot() Sunday branch upgraded from generic deferred stub to actionable omission rubric — Sunday Small Vespers ordinarily omits kathisma; Kathisma 1 belongs to Saturday Great Vespers vigil; resolvedAs:'sunday-small-vespers-kathisma-ordinary-omitted'. (4) troparion-or-apolytikion: _resolveTroparionSlot() Sunday branch upgraded from deferred rubric to type:'text' with full Resurrectional Troparion of the tone; resolvedAs:'resurrectional-troparion-sunday'. (5) theotokion-dismissal: _resolveTheotokionSlot() Sunday branch upgraded from deferred rubric to type:'text' with full dismissal Theotokion of the tone; resolvedAs:'dismissal-theotokion-sunday'. data/horologion/octoechos-vespers.json: unchanged — sunday_deferred:true entries remain as data markers; routing override is in engine only. data/horologion/vespers-kathisma.json, vespers-troparion.json, vespers-theotokion.json: unchanged. office-ui.js: NO CHANGES — type:'text' and type:'stichera' already handled. GOVERNANCE CONSEQUENCE: The 'Sunday Vespers bundle deferred' framing is retired. Every Vespers variable slot across all day-of-week paths now yields full text, explicit rubric stub, or explicit omission rubric. Remaining work is refinement: weekday Menaion refinement (Mon–Fri troparion/theotokion text) and feast-rank override system.

change_history.change: Saints data migration — tradition string to tags[] array schema
change_history.detail: All 1568 saint records across 12 monthly files migrated. Legacy `tradition` string field replaced with: id (stable slug), type (saint/apostle/prophet/feast/marian_feast/commemoration), tags[] (ANG/LAT/EOR/OOR/COE), verification_status (verified/provisional). 137 formerly-Universal entries fully classified via external research and editorial review. Mechanical renames: Catholic->LAT, Anglican Communion->ANG, Eastern Orthodox->EOR, Oriental Orthodox->OOR, Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo->OOR, Byzantine Orthodox->EOR, Church of the East->COE. Multi-tradition comma-separated strings parsed and split correctly. Barbara/John of Damascus combined entry split into saint-barbara-of-nicomedia (EOR/LAT/OOR) and saint-john-of-damascus (ANG/EOR/LAT). Conception of the BVM split: Dec 8 feast-immaculate-conception (ANG/LAT) and Dec 9 feast-conception-of-the-theotokos (EOR/OOR). ECU never stored — derived dynamically at render time when all five tags present. JS filter logic in office-ui.js requires update (next task).

change_history.change: COE saint section silenced pending architectural rebuild
change_history.detail: esySaints filter and saint-display block removed from renderEastSyriac(). saint-section hidden, saint-display cleared. Previous string-matching behaviour was historically inaccurate for the East Syriac tradition. COE calendar is salvation-historical and seasonal — not a date-to-saint grid. Correct rebuild requires three-layer model (season engine, fixed feasts, sparse saints). Full spec in known_outstanding_issues.coe_calendar_model. Silence is liturgically accurate for COE weekdays with no fixed individual saint.

change_history.change: Horologion v1.4 — Monday–Friday baseline Octoechos stichera/aposticha materially populated
change_history.detail: data/horologion/octoechos-vespers.json: version bumped 1.3.0 → 1.4.0. Replaced baseline_stub:true entries for Monday–Friday with fully populated stichera_at_lord_i_have_cried and aposticha objects (coverage:'full-baseline') for all 8 tones. 80 weekday slots populated (8 tones × 5 days × 2 slots); 240 verses total (3 per slot). Thematic assignments per standard Byzantine Octoechos practice: Monday=Bodiless Powers (Angels), Tuesday=St. John the Baptist and the Prophets, Wednesday=Cross and Theotokos, Thursday=Holy Apostles and St. Nicholas, Friday=Cross and Theotokos (penitential). Sunday entries: updated from bare baseline_stub to sunday_deferred:true + baseline_stub:true with an explicit note explaining that Saturday great Vespers already provides the Sunday-tone resurrectional content, and Sunday small Vespers ordinary stichera are a distinct corpus deferred to a future pass. This is an explicit deferral, not a silent gap. js/horologion-engine.js: NO CHANGES in this pass. The existing _resolveSticheraSlot() three-tier logic (full data → stub → null) already handles the newly populated weekday entries without modification. office-ui.js: NO CHANGES. Saturday data: unchanged from v1.3. NET: Monday–Friday Vespers stichera and aposticha are now materially resolved for all 8 tones. Sunday remains an honest stub. kathisma-reading, troparion-or-apolytikion, theotokion-dismissal: unchanged placeholders.

change_history.change: Horologion v1.5 — kathisma-reading baseline resolved for ordinary Monday–Saturday Vespers

Rule: standard Byzantine Psalter weekly cycle for ordinary Vespers (OCA/Antiochian Psalter table; Jordanville Horologion 2008). Assignments (LXX psalm numbers): Monday=Kathisma 4 (Ps 24–31), Tuesday=6 (37–45), Wednesday=8 (55–63), Thursday=10 (70–76), Friday=12 (85–90, 92), Saturday=1 (1–8). Sunday entry: sunday_deferred:true with honest note about usage variation. js/horologion-engine.js: version bumped v1.3→v1.5. Friday entry includes variant_note about Ps 91/92 edition differences. All three cases state their limitations openly in rendered text — no silent approximations. Sunday kathisma remains an honest stub. troparion-or-apolytikion and theotokion-dismissal: unchanged placeholders.

change_history.change: Horologion v1.9 — weekday Vespers dismissal Theotokion (Mon–Fri) resolved from Octoechos weekday corpus
change_history.detail: New file: data/horologion/vespers-weekday-theotokion.json. 40 entries keyed by tone string ('1'–'8') then weekday name ('monday'–'friday'). Each entry: tone, weekday, title, full liturgical text. Monday/Tuesday/Thursday: standard Octoechos weekday Theotokion for that tone + day. Wednesday/Friday: Stavrotheotokion (Cross Theotokion) — standard Byzantine fasting-day practice. js/horologion-engine.js: version bumped v1.8→v1.9. WEEKDAY_THEOTOKION_URL constant added. _weekdayTheotokionData cache variable added. _loadWeekdayTheotokionData(): lazy fetch, non-throwing, graceful degradation — if data file fails, falls back to v1.7-style tone-identified rubric stub (not a crash). _resolveTheotokionSlot() weekday branch (Mon–Fri): upgraded from tone-identified rubric stub (resolvedAs:'weekday-menaion-required') to type:'text' with full Octoechos weekday Theotokion text (resolvedAs:'weekday-octoechos-theotokion'). Limitation recorded in each output: baseline uses current Octoechos weekly tone; if Menaion troparion is of a different tone the Theotokion should follow that tone — not yet implemented; stated openly. _capitalise() internal helper added for weekday name formatting in rubric text. Promise.all in _resolveVespersSlots: five-loader → six-loader (adds _loadWeekdayTheotokionData()). Saturday, Sunday, and Bright Week theotokion paths: UNCHANGED. troparion-or-apolytikion (Mon–Fri): UNCHANGED — remains tone-identified rubric stub; Menaion data is genuinely required; no fabrication. office-ui.js: NO CHANGES — type:'text' already handled. GOVERNANCE CONSEQUENCE: The framing 'weekday Menaion refinement for troparion + theotokion' is now incorrect. After v1.9, the only remaining open weekday slot is troparion-or-apolytikion (Mon–Fri); theotokion-dismissal is baseline-resolved. The term 'weekday Menaion refinement' now refers exclusively to weekday troparion text.

change_history.change: Menaion v1 pilot wired into live weekday Vespers troparion resolution. Engine version v2.1.
change_history.detail: NEW FILES: js/menaion-resolver.js — IIFE module (window.MenaionResolver). Soft dependency on horologion-engine.js. Exposes queryTroparion(mmdd) → Promise<ResultObject>. MONTH_FILES map: only month 11 (november) active. _loadMonth(): lazy fetch, non-throwing, result cached. _selectBestCommemoration(): highest rank (lowest integer); ties go to first entry. Result status codes: menaion-resolved, menaion-text-unavailable, menaion-not-imported, menaion-no-ranked-commemoration, menaion-load-error. data/menaion/schema.json: pilot schema contract for all future monthly Menaion files. data/menaion/november.json: 12 of 30 November dates imported (11-01, 11-08, 11-09, 11-13, 11-14, 11-16, 11-17, 11-20, 11-21, 11-22, 11-25, 11-30). Source: OCA/Antiochian Hapgood-tradition English; Jordanville Horologion. CHANGED FILES: index.html — js/menaion-resolver.js added before js/horologion-engine.js. js/horologion-engine.js — version bumped v2.0→v2.1. _resolveTroparionSlot(): made async; third parameter dateObj added. Bright Week, Saturday, Sunday branches: UNCHANGED. Weekday (Mon–Fri) branch: builds MM-DD string from dateObj; calls window.MenaionResolver.queryTroparion(mmdd); routes on status: menaion-resolved → type:'text' with full troparion text + saint name + tone + rank annotation (resolvedAs:'menaion-resolved'); menaion-text-unavailable → type:'rubric' with commemoration name (resolvedAs:'menaion-text-unavailable'); all other statuses or resolver unavailable → falls through to weekday-theme rubric (resolvedAs:'weekday-theme-rubric', identical to v2.0 fallback). try/catch wraps entire resolver call — non-throwing. typeof window.MenaionResolver guard prevents crash if module not loaded. Call site in _resolveVespersSlots(): _resolveTroparionSlot() awaited with dateObj. _resolveVespersSlots() was already async — no signature change. office-ui.js: NO CHANGES. PILOT SCOPE: November only (12 of 30 dates). All other months return 'menaion-not-imported'. GOVERNANCE CONSEQUENCE: The framing 'weekday troparion is simply unresolved' is retired. After Menaion v1, weekday troparion has a live pilot path. Imported November dates resolve to full text. Non-imported dates fall back honestly to the theme rubric. Remaining work is corpus expansion + feast-rank engine. 'Menaion required' now refers to corpus expansion, not a missing architecture.

rubric_loop_handlers_bcp: VARIABLE_OPENING — resolves bcp-opening-${season}, fallback to bcp-opening-general
VARIABLE_ANTIPHON — resolves antiphon_mp or antiphon_ep from dailyData
VARIABLE_PSALM — renders appointed psalms with optional Gloria Patri
VARIABLE_READING_OT — OT lesson from independent morning or evening chain
VARIABLE_READING_EPISTLE — Epistle from independent morning or evening chain
VARIABLE_READING_GOSPEL — Gospel subject to gospelPlacement setting
VARIABLE_COLLECT — collect lookup with manual ID mappings; triggers Examen and Kyrie Pantocrator
VARIABLE_WEEKDAY_COLLECT — tiered fallback: dailyData.collect_weekday → bcp-collect-grace/peace → skip

VARIABLE_READING_ET — canonical ET-tradition scripture reading; resolves ethHourInfo.etReading; active for Nigatu (1 Clement) and both Le'lit watches (Shepherd of Hermas); silently skipped for all other hours
eth-introduction-to-every-hour — (v2.6.1) renders rubric_before as italic rubric label, then full Tselote Meweta text (Lord's Prayer + Thanksgiving + Psalm 50/51). Logs warning if component missing.
eth-saatat-hour-slot — resolves canonical hour text from getEthiopianHourInfo().hourId; injects italicised Sixth Hour commemorative sentence when hourId === 'eth-lika-hour-text'
eth-mazmur-slot — renders appointed Psalms via ethHourInfo.psalms; closes with eth-anqasa-birhan. Full edition prepends 12-fold Leke Haile chant.

eth-saints-commemoration — FULL SENKESSAR PIPELINE: calls EthiopianCalendar.getEthiopianDate(), lazy-loads and caches senkessar-index.json, fetches and caches per-day narrative from data/synaxarium/ethiopian/{monthSlug}/{day}.json. Falls back to Oriental saints filter, then generic intercession. MONTH_SLUG_MAP handles miyazya→miazia.
rubric_loop_handlers_east_syriac: esy-variable-seasonal-onitha — selects from esy-onitha-lent (sauma), esy-onitha-easter (qyamta), or esy-onitha-ordinary
esy-variable-qutaa-psalms — fixed Third Hour selection: Psalms 19, 24, 25 [v2.8.0]
esy-variable-endana-psalms — fixed Sixth Hour selection: Psalms 119:1-32, 121, 122 [v2.8.0]
esy-variable-dtsha-sain-psalms — fixed Ninth Hour selection: Psalms 84, 116, 117 [v2.8.0]
esy-variable-marmitha — selects 3 psalms from marmithaMap[cycle][dayOfWeek]. Qdham: Pss 1–21; Wathar: Pss 22–42
esy-variable-hulali — selects psalms from hulaliMap[cycle][dayOfWeek]. Night office psalmody covering full Psalter

esy-variable-qanona — [v2.8.1 UPDATED] For Sapra, Ramsha, Lelya: renders hour-specific Qanona (esy-qanona-{hour}) first, then seasonal Qanona (esy-qanona-{season}-{cycle}). For all other hours: seasonal Qanona only. Fallback: esy-qanona-ordinary.
esy-variable-onitha-with-qala — renders Qala tone marker then Onitha text (monastic sequences)
esy-variable-barekmar-intercessions — [v2.8.1] day-specific diptychs (esy-barekmar-{day}); sauma seasonal form (esy-barekmar-sauma); fallback esy-barekmar-general

private_methods: _getEaster(year) — Anonymous Gregorian algorithm; returns JS Date of Easter Sunday for any year
_getAdventSunday(year) — returns JS Date of Advent Sunday (Sunday on or after Nov 27) for any year
_generateSeasonRanges(year) — generates all season range objects for the liturgical year beginning in Nov/Dec of `year`
_getRangesForDate(date) — returns the correct generated range set for any date; checks prior and current calendar year
_julianToGregorianOffset(year) — century-based Julian→Gregorian offset helper for EO fixed-feast diagnostics
_isoFromDate(date) — local-time-safe YYYY-MM-DD formatter used by EO diagnostics
